headline: How Much Upside Can NVDA Stock's Growth Deliver?
summary: At $195.74, NVIDIA (NVDA) looks set up for roughly 51% of upside over the next three years under a conservative scenario. That is a move large enough to justify digging into where it comes from. Revenue compounding does the work, but the multiple takes a meaningful cut along the way. Here is the operational reality the math is built on.

headline: Qualcomm Just Promised $15 Billion in AI Chip Sales, but There’s a Catch: The Chips Don’t Exist Yet

headline: Micron Smashed Even the Bulls: Evercore Says This AI Rally Has the ‘Earnings Power’ to Last

headline: Chips Throw a Party, Apple Sends the Bill, and Hormuz Closes the Tap
summary: Micron's blowout guide pulled the rest of the chip complex up Thursday, but Apple's price-hike admission and a fresh shutdown of tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz kept the broader tape honest. Nasdaq 100 led, the Composite leaked, and oil pushed back above $75.

headline: We are in a race for chips and memory, says ODDO BHF's Stéphane Houri
summary: Memory prices are likely to remain elevated for the next two to three years, driven by continued AI investment and capital spending from hyperscalers, according to Stéphane Houri, Head of Equity Research at ODDO BHF. Houri also argues that AI investment opportunities are expanding far beyond the likes of Nvidia, which is nearly flat year to date, to a broader range of sectors, including power, connectivity and CPUs.

headline: AMD (AMD) Acquires MEXT To Tackle AI Memory Bottlenecks In Data Centers
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NasdaqGS:AMD) has agreed to acquire MEXT, a company focused on AI driven memory optimization. The deal is aimed at addressing AI memory bottlenecks in data centers by integrating predictive memory technology into AMD’s platform. This move is positioned to broaden AMD’s data center capabilities and support enterprise AI infrastructure customers. For investors watching AMD’s push into data center and enterprise AI, the MEXT acquisition focuses on one of the less talked...
